description: Expected difference in out-of-sample and in-sample Sharpe ratio of an asset pricing model. The figure plots the expected difference between the out-of-sample Sharpe ratio and the in-sample Sharpe ratio, , as a function of the population Sharpe ratio, θ, for an asset pricing model with N traded factors and an estimation window with T periods. Plots for two different values of N (3 and 6) and T (120 and 240) are presented.

Density of in-sample Sharpe ratio of an asset pricing model (T = 120). The figure plots the density of  of an asset pricing model with N traded factors when the length of estimation window is T = 120, where  is the in-sample Sharpe ratio and θ is the population Sharpe ratio. Plots for two different values of number of traded factors (N = 3 and 6) and population Sharpe ratio (θ = 0.2 and 0.4) are presented.

Density of out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of an asset pricing model (T = 120). The figure plots the density of  of an asset pricing model with N traded factors when the length of estimation window is T = 120, where  is the out-of-sample Sharpe ratio and θ is the population Sharpe ratio. Plots for two different values of number of traded factors (N = 3 and 6) and population Sharpe ratio (θ = 0.2 and 0.4) are presented.

Conditional density of out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of an asset pricing model (T = 120). The figure plots the conditional density of the normalized out-of-sample Sharpe ratio () of an asset pricing model when conditional on the in-sample Sharpe ratio () is at its 10th (solid line), 50th (dotted line), and 90th (dashed line) percentiles. The length of estimation window is T = 120 and plots for two different values of number of traded factors (N = 3 and 6) and population Sharpe ratio (θ = 0.2 and 0.4) are presented.

Exact and approximate densities of in-sample Sharpe ratio of an asset pricing model (T = 120). The figure plots the exact density (solid line) of the normalized in-sample Sharpe ratio (θˆ/θ) and two different approximated density, the first one assumes N is fixed and T → ∞ (dashed line), and the second one assumes both N and T → ∞ with N/T → ρ (dotted line). The length of time series is T = 120 and plots for two different values of number of traded factors (N = 3 and 6) and population Sharpe ratio (θ = 0.2 and 0.4) are presented.

Exact and approximate densities of out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of an asset pricing model (T = 120). The figure plots the exact density (solid line) of the normalized out-of-sample Sharpe ratio (θ˜/θ) and two different approximated density, the first one assumes N is fixed and T → ∞ (dashed line), and the second one assumes both N and T → ∞ with N/T → ρ (dotted line). The length of time series is T = 120 and plots for two different values of number of traded factors (N = 3 and 6) and population Sharpe ratio (θ = 0.2 and 0.4) are presented.
